x-enumeration-literals.name: Fund Movement
x-enumeration-literals.description: Transfers money between accounts, customers, or financial institutions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: International Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Transfers of money between parties in different countries.

Comment:
Examples:

SWIFT Transfers  banks sending money internationally using the SWIFT network.

Western Union or MoneyGram  cash-based remittances to other countries.

Wise (formerly TransferWise)  online platform offering cheaper currency exchange and transfers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mobile Money Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Transfers made via mobile wallets or mobile banking apps, often without needing a traditional bank account.

Comment:
Examples:

M-Pesa in Kenya  users send and receive money via mobile phones.

GCash in the Philippines  mobile wallet for transfers and payments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Remittance Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Transfers where the recipient receives cash at a designated location, instead of into a bank account.

Comment:
Examples:

Western Union Cash Pickup  recipient collects cash at an agent location.

MoneyGram  allows worldwide cash pickups.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Transfer Processing
x-enumeration-literals.description: Handles the initiation, authorization, and settlement of transfer transactions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card-Based Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Transfers using debit or credit cards, often through online services or ATMs.

Comment:
Examples:

Visa Direct  sends money directly to a recipients debit card.

Mastercard Send  instant person-to-person or business-to-consumer payments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cryptocurrency Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Digital currency transfers using blockchain networks.

Comment:
Examples:

Sending Bitcoin or Ethereum between wallets.

Using USDT (Tether) for stable-value transfers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Notification Transfer Service
x-enumeration-literals.description: Notification Cash Transfer Service is a service where the bank or financial system sends alerts or notifications to the parties involved in a cash transfer.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Account Verification
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ensures the senders account has sufficient funds and validates the recipients account details.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Security and Authorization
x-enumeration-literals.description: Applies authentication, fraud detection, and approval mechanisms to ensure secure transfers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Security and Authorization
x-enumeration-literals.description: Applies authentication, fraud detection, and approval mechanisms to ensure secure transfers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Transaction Tracking and Reporting
x-enumeration-literals.description: Records transfer details for monitoring, reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.
